definition:
1. to seize underneath  or inwardly , (Homer) 
2. to draw  or take away from under  a thing, with genitive, (Iliad by Homer):—;also τὴν χεῖρα ὑφῄρει tried to draw  it away , (Aristophanes Comicus) 
3. to take away underhand, filch away , (Thucydides); ὑφ. τῆς ὑποψίας gradually to take away  part of . ., (Thucydides):—;Pass., ὑπαραιρημένος (ionic perfect participle) put secretly away, made away with , (Herdotus Historicus):—;so in Mid. to take away underhand, filch away, purloin , (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
4. in Mid. also with accusative pers., ὑφ. τινά τινος to rob  him of a thing, (Aeschines Orator) (ML)
